scope: Sequence-packing audit for ALM 14B training — stacked on top of the 7 r6 speedup patches
target_round: r7 (packing is NOT required for r6 — see verdict)
verdict: NOOP — current trainer already achieves 0% padding via token-stream concatenation. Sequence packing is a near-zero uplift.

corpus_measurement.sample_notes: Production corpus is /workspace/anima/training/corpus_large.txt on the H100 pod; we measured the local seed (~63KB) and assume the large corpus has a similar length distribution because both are scraped from the same Korean paragraph/dialog/Q&A mix.
corpus_measurement.tokenizer_assumption: Qwen/Qwen2.5-14B-Instruct BPE (local tokenizer not invoked). Estimation: 1 token ≈ 1.2 Korean chars (empirical Qwen2 Korean BPE ratio, verified via prior measurements).
corpus_measurement.length_distribution_summary: STRONGLY short-seq dominated — p99 doc length (310 tok) is 3.3x shorter than seq_len=1024. No doc reaches the seq boundary. This is the EXACT profile where sequence packing typically delivers 2-4x wall-clock speedup over naive right-pad — IF the trainer right-pads. It does not.

behavior: load_corpus_chunked() reads file in 10MB chunks, tokenizes EACH CHUNK as raw text (tokenizer treats chunk content as continuous text, ignoring doc boundaries).
torch.cat concatenates all chunk token tensors into ONE 1D tensor of shape (N_total_tokens,).
TextDataset.__getitem__(idx) returns tokens[idx : idx + block_size + 1] and splits into (x, y) — every sample is EXACTLY block_size tokens long.
n_samples = len(tokens) - block_size - 1 — a dense sliding window over the concatenated stream.
current_packing_state.pad_token_usage: tokenizer.pad_token is set on line 167-168 only to satisfy HF tokenizer config — never actually consumed in the data pipeline (no DataCollator, no attention_mask, no right-pad).
current_packing_state.equivalent_to: A maximally aggressive form of 'sequence packing' — effectively identical to TRL SFTTrainer(packing=True) but WITHOUT attention-mask doc boundaries. It is slightly MORE efficient (no mask ops) and slightly LESS semantically clean (cross-doc attention is not masked).

expected_uplift_range.pessimistic_if_packing_broken_added: -3 to -8% (adding attention_mask reintroduces kernel overhead; packing=True via TRL SFTTrainer would force a trainer rewrite and re-add masked softmax cost)

chosen_strategy: NOOP — DO NOT ADD SEQUENCE PACKING
rationale: The existing TextDataset + load_corpus_chunked path is ALREADY a maximally efficient form of sequence packing (dense sliding window over a concatenated token stream).
Padding ratio is 0.0000 — there is no wasted FLOPs-on-pad to reclaim.

The 7 speedup patches in flight for r6 (flash_attention, disable_act_ckpt, torch_compile, fused_adamw, dataloader, nccl_env, tf32_cudnn) deliver the full ~50% conservative uplift. Stacking a 'packing' layer on top returns ~0% and risks introducing attention_mask / position_ids handling regressions.

Any switch to TRL SFTTrainer(packing=True) would be a STRICT REGRESSION: it introduces per-sample attention masking + position-id reset, which adds kernel overhead, replaces the current custom loop (which is well-tuned for peft+FSDP+grad-ckpt), and removes the --target-modules / --unfreeze-embed / --unfreeze-lm-head / --doc-separator / --shuffle-docs / --dedupe r5 flags.

The only potential 'packing' gain would be a QUALITY improvement (adding EOS + reset_position_ids + attention_mask at doc boundaries to prevent cross-doc contamination). That is NOT a speed patch — it's a capability patch, and belongs to a different audit track (quality, not MFU).
Risk/reward: expected uplift 0%, implementation effort ~200 lines, risk of breaking --doc-separator/--shuffle-docs semantics on the patched pod trainer. Ratio is negative.

risk_factors: Pod trainer has --doc-separator / --shuffle-docs / --dedupe flags NOT present in local train_alm_14b.py — local audit cannot see the exact pod state without ssh, so any packing patch written against the local file would miss the pod divergence and likely conflict on apply.
TRL SFTTrainer swap requires peft+FSDP compatibility re-validation — r5's use_orig_params=True path is known-good with the current custom loop.
Re-tokenizing the corpus offline into a packed dataset would violate the 'no re-tokenize on running pod' rule.

alternative_quality_recommendation_defer.description: If document-boundary quality becomes a concern in a future round (r8+), the correct minimal change is: (1) insert tokenizer.eos_token_id between documents during load_corpus_chunked (already partially done by r5 --doc-separator on the pod), (2) optionally add an attention_mask that resets at EOS, and (3) optionally reset position_ids at EOS. This is a quality patch, not a speed patch, and is EXPLICITLY OUT OF SCOPE for this audit (which is MFU-focused).
alternative_quality_recommendation_defer.tracked_as: follow-up quality ticket (not created by this audit)

sign_off.rule_do_not_retokenize_on_running_pod: honored — all measurement done on LOCAL seed corpus
sign_off.rule_l0_untouched: honored — no files in anima/core/ modified
sign_off.rule_hexa_first_exempt: honored — .py file audit only, no new .py created

deliverables.patch_file: NONE — NOOP verdict, no diff produced

summary_for_caller: Current TextDataset is already a dense token-stream slider — padding_ratio=0.0000. Sequence packing NOOP. Expected r7 uplift from packing: 0%. Stick with the 7 r6 speedup patches. If quality becomes a concern later, consider EOS reset as a SEPARATE quality-only patch (out of this audit's scope).
